\h 8 Enhancement Revision History Changed By Date Version Notes: Mitchell Bluford 02/ 2 5 /202 2 0.0 Original document. EX 2 9 – P50 Unit Level Case Pick Replenishment Allocation Changes In certain areas of the warehouse, MCLN stores case items in locations that do not fit entire pallets. These locations are known as “Case Flow” locations. MCLN C ase F low locations are replenished at a unit level . E ach unit is equivalent to one pack per MCLN design . Base P50 Unit Level Allocation generation will surpass the Pick Location’s Maximum Inventory and overfill the location if the wave’s demand surpassed this maximum . This is unacceptable for MCLN operations. For this reason, EX29 is customized to ensure that each individual P50 allocation will be generated at no more than Pick Location inventory maximum, while the sum still satisfies the demand from the wave. After this allocation split, base Task Creation Rules can easily split the allocations onto separate tasks to be released and completed as space becomes available in the location. Summary Information Customer Name: McLane Customer Code: MCLN Subproject Codes: TBD Change Request Number: Overview Functional Area Enhanced Additional Information User Interface (UI) N MHE N RF N Reporting N Other N Assumptions This extension only changes INT-9 allocations replenishing at the Unit UOM . Pick Location Max is configured and valid on locations, else will be assumed at the value configuration in custom code C-E29 byte 1-3. This value has to be numeric in between 1-999 . The database does not accept non-numeric values in the LOCN_HDR.MAX_INVN_QTY field, so the only values which are considered not valid here are 0 and 1. If either of these values are passed, EX29 will assume LOCN_HDR.MAX_INVN_QTY=C-E29-E29 byte 1-3 (this value is configured). There is no change to the total sum of P50 allocations, this extension simply splits them across new records with any individual record never pulling more than the max of the location . Base code will still generate the original P50 allocation . There are no changes to base Task Creation Rules in this extension. To be more specific, it is still necessary to break these replenishment tasks by a volume that will ensure each Allocation Inventory Detail will end up on only one task . If we have volume set too high on a P50 allocation going to a Pick Location Determination zone enabled f or this behavior, there is still the possibility that it will be combined. This extension excludes c igarettes from this behavior . EX05 tiered replenishment can still overfill a location. An INT-9 created to be then used for an EX05 INT-3 cut-down replenishment can still overfill the Case Pick location. This INT-9 would be considered a ‘P30’ replenishment and therefore excluded from the behavior outlined in this spec. The most granular level of enablement in this extension is the Pick Location Determination Zone. A PDZ must either have this behavior enabled or disabled. Validations Validations The following section describes the custom flags required to enable this portion of the extension. First, the global modifications flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID 000 parameter byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the next enablement validation. Otherwise, if this flag is set to ‘N’, the global extension control is turned off, and only base processing will occur. Second, the extension specific global flag must be enabled. This is controlled through the custom system code C000 code ID EX29 byte 1-1. This parameter is configured as a Y/N flag. If the flag is set to ‘Y’, then WMOS continues to the custom processing below. Otherwise, if the flag is set to ‘N’, the extension specific control for EX29 is turned off, and no custom EX29 processing will occur. Last, as the procedure fetches the applicable INT-9 Allocation Inventory Detail records, WMOS will check to ensure that the Destination Location of the records has custom byte 97-97 “EX29 Hard Location Maximum” of B-331 Pick Location Determination Zones set to ‘Y’. If this flag is enabled , then the record will be returned in our set of data and split across the maximum. If this flag is not set to ‘Y’ then custom functionality will be disabled and the P50 allocation will not be split. Updates During waving, before task creation, this procedure will be triggered. The procedure will target INT-9 allocation records at the U nit UOM, in priority 50, are non-tiered cut-down (see assumption #7) , are non-cigarette, and where the quantity allocated on the task is higher than the maximum inventory quantity of the Pick Location . Once these records are found, WMOS will begin creating new AID records, each with the quantity allocated being equal to the maximum inventory of the location. The last AID record created could have the quantity allocated be less than the maximum of the location, so that the sum across new records will exactly match the quantity allocated on the original AID record. If 0 or 1 is passed as the Pick Location Maximum, EX29 will treat this as if the Pick Location has not been properly configured. In this event, EX29 will update the Pick Location Max (only in this procedure – no changes will be made to actual Pick Location M ax) to the sys value configured in system code C-E29-E29 byte 1-3 “Default Pick Locn Max”. There are no other changes in behavior other than that this value will be assumed for these locations. Dependencies Code Dependencies N/A Interface Dependencies Interface Field Requirement P latform Information Solution: Solution Platform: Solution Version: Warehouse Management Open Systems Oracle/ Tomcat /RHEL 201 9 Proces s Flow Diagram Process Steps UI No UI changes in scope for this extension. MHE Outputs Activity Tracking PIX No changes to base behavior for PIX generation as part of this extension. LM Hooks Not in scope as part of this extension. RF No RF changes in scope for this extension. Process Change/Addition No additional process changes in scope for this extension. Reporting , Label, or Document Not report, label or document changes in scope for this extension. Other Solutions Included in Extension Not in scope for this extension. Performance Expectations The p urpose of this section is to identify performance requirements up front Process Step Peak Transaction Rate Max Response 95% Response Recovery Queue Recovery Time Operations Driver & notes Required Data Setup All necessary data setup for the extension to function accurately are detailed below.
